deviations.description: The change from a closed interval [66,) to '>65' for patient age is semantically equivalent; both capture patients older than 65. There is no compliance deviation.
The process model does not require discharge approval for patients with more than 5 medications. This violates the new requirement that mandates doctor approval for such patients.

The process model requires a nurse to approve discharge for patients over 65, but the new requirement mandates doctor approval. The process violates the stricter requirement by not involving a doctor.
The process model enforces nurse approval for discharge, but the new requirement no longer requires nurse approval. The process unnecessarily restricts discharge by involving a nurse.

The process model enforces a 24-hour time constraint for nurse approval before discharge, but the new requirement does not specify any time constraint. The process unnecessarily restricts discharge timing.
The change in temporal validity only shifts the rule's active period and does not affect the process model's compliance logic.
